name: openalex
description: OpenAlex scholarly database — query research papers (works), authors, institutions, topics, sources, publishers, funders, keywords, and more. DOI lookup, citation graphs, OA-PDF download, bibliometrics. Vendored from the google-deepmind/science-skills bundle.
author: zorai

OPENALEX_API_KEY.label: OpenAlex API key (optional, raises rate limit)
OPENALEX_API_KEY.description: Optional. Get one at https://openalex.org/. Without a key, requests are anonymous and rate-limited to ~10 req/s. With one, the limit is higher and the polite-pool header is set. Loaded by the script via dotenv from ~/.env.

rate-limit.description: Check current OpenAlex rate-limit status (polls the rate-limit endpoint; reports remaining / reset).

resolve.description: Resolve an entity name to its OpenAlex ID. Entity types: works, authors, sources, institutions, topics, domains, fields, subfields, sdgs, countries, continents, languages, keywords, publishers, funders.

python.command: uv run scripts/openalex_cli.py resolve "${OA_ENTITY_TYPE:?set OA_ENTITY_TYPE}" "${OA_QUERY:?set OA_QUERY}" ${OA_PER_PAGE:+--per-page $OA_PER_PAGE}

get.description: Get a single entity by its OpenAlex ID (e.g. W2741809807 for a work, A123 for an author).

python.command: uv run scripts/openalex_cli.py get "${OA_ENTITY_TYPE:?set OA_ENTITY_TYPE}" "${OA_ID:?set OA_ID}" ${OA_SELECT:+--select $OA_SELECT}

filter.description: Filter and search entities. Most common: works with --search, --filter, --sort, --group-by, --per-page.

download-pdf.description: ⚠ COSTS $0.01 PER REQUEST. Download the OA-PDF for a work to the given output path. Confirm with the user before invoking in bulk.

python.command: uv run scripts/openalex_cli.py download-pdf "${OA_ID:?set OA_ID (OpenAlex Work ID, e.g. W2741809807)}" "${OA_OUTPUT:?set OA_OUTPUT (output file path, e.g. paper.pdf)}"

run.description: Generic catch-all: forward an arbitrary openalex_cli.py invocation. Use for any of the 5 subcommands not explicitly named (currently all 5 are exposed as named commands, so this is mostly for forward-compat and full-arg-passing).
